01   About Me

Who I Am

I'm someone who is always curious — about how systems work, why things fail, and what can be built better. I grew up in India, moved to Latvia to study, and spent time in Germany on Erasmus. Living across three countries shaped how I think: adaptable, observant, and never afraid of the unfamiliar.

My passion in IT is cybersecurity — especially finding vulnerabilities, understanding how attacks happen, and building defences. My BSc thesis explored using AI and LLMs for vulnerability detection in system logs, which is exactly the kind of research that excites me. I also love building things — websites, tools, and one day, apps.

Outside of tech, I paint, draw, cook, hit the gym, play football, and get behind the wheel whenever I can. I believe the best engineers are well-rounded humans, not just code machines.

🌍

3 Countries, 1 Career

India → Latvia → Germany (Erasmus). Each country taught me something different about people, problems, and persistence.

🔍

I Like Finding What's Broken

Whether it's a security gap, a workflow inefficiency, or a broken process — my instinct is always to find it and fix it.

🤝

I Can Convince People

Trained 10+ people at KFC, advised insurance clients in India — I know how to communicate complex things simply and get people on board.

03   Projects

What I've Built

01
// BSc Thesis   2025–2026

LLM Log Anomaly Detection System

Designed and implemented a full experimental pipeline comparing traditional ML vs large language models for detecting anomalies in HDFS system logs. Processed 11 million+ log lines, trained a TF-IDF + Logistic Regression classifier, then evaluated Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Llama 3.1 8B across three prompt strategies. Also conducted hallucination and consistency analysis.

93.58% F1 Score
11M+ Log Lines
100% Consistency
20% Hallucination Rate
Python scikit-learn OpenRouter API Cerebras API LaTeX HDFS Dataset
02
// Security Lab   2025

Linux File Permissions Security Audit

Performed a complete Linux file system security audit, identifying misconfigured permissions and applying correct access controls using chmod, chown, and ls -la. Documented findings in a professional security portfolio following industry-standard reporting practices.

Linux Bash chmod Security Hardening
03
// Personal   Coming Soon

Portfolio Website

This site. Built with pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no frameworks, no templates. Designed from scratch with a cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic, custom cursor, scroll animations, and skill bars. Hosted on GitHub Pages.

HTML CSS JavaScript GitHub Pages
